title: Sour Fluffy Brazilian Cassava Fritters
description: A light, airy fritter that balances the earthy sweetness of cassava with a tangy citrus kick and a drizzle of rich treacle.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the grated cassava, baking powder, and sea salt.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg, then stir in the coconut milk, tamarind paste, and treacle until well combined.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry mixture and stir until a thick batter forms; let it rest for 5 minutes.
While the batter rests, heat the vegetable oil in a deep skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Drop spoonfuls of the batter into the hot oil, pressing lightly to form small discs.
Cook the fritters for 3–4 minutes on each side, until golden brown and puffed.
Remove the fritters with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve warm, optionally drizzled with extra treacle and a squeeze of fresh lime.
